title: The Power of Geography: Ten Maps That Reveal the Future of Our World
excerpt: The sequel to Prisoners of Geography, examining ten more regions where geography will shape the defining geopolitical struggles of the 21st century, from the Arctic to the South China Sea.
keyIdeas: Geography continues to shape the emerging geopolitical order of the 21st century
New frontiers — the Arctic, space, the oceans — are becoming strategic battlegrounds
Climate change is reshaping geographic realities, opening new routes and creating new pressures
keyTakeaways: The Arctic is becoming a new strategic arena as ice melts, opening shipping routes and resource access
Space is the ultimate high ground, and control of orbital assets is increasingly contested
Australia's geographic isolation is both a vulnerability and a strategic asset
The South China Sea is the most dangerous flashpoint in the world due to its strategic chokepoints
The Sahel region is becoming a zone of cascading crises driven by climate and population pressure
whoShouldRead: Readers of Prisoners of Geography wanting the sequel
Anyone interested in the emerging geopolitical challenges of the 21st century
Students of international relations and strategic studies
Policy analysts and professionals working in international affairs
whoShouldSkip: Those who haven't read Prisoners of Geography (start there)
Readers seeking analysis of domestic politics rather than international relations
People looking for optimistic predictions about global cooperation
